summary: UAV activity is reported across multiple Ukrainian regions, including north, south, east and central oblasts, with several groups heading toward key towns. Russia is reported to control Novovasylivka in Kharkiv region and threatens Iskander-M missile launches from Voronezh. Sweden will deliver 16 Gripen fighters to Ukraine, with deliveries expected in 2027.
key_points: UAV groups from Belarus and Russian regions are heading north into Chernihiv and Kharkiv oblasts, targeting towns such as Mena and Chuhuiv (Source: @kpszsu/63124).
New UAV groups reported from Nikopol in the south and west, indicating expanded southern air activity (Source: @kpszsu/63128).
Russian forces have taken control of Novovasylivka in Kharkiv region (Source: @tass_agency/377951).
Threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Voronezh region noted (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67252).
Sweden commits to supplying Ukraine with 16 Gripen fighters, delivery slated for early 2027 (Source: @UkraineNow/81512).
Ukrainian defense budget increased by 1.56 trillion hryvnias, funded by EU assistance (Source: @UkraineNow/81510).
UAVs observed over the Black Sea heading toward the southern coast and Odesa direction (Source: @kpszsu/63140).

sections.executive_summary: UAV activity intensifies across Ukraine with multiple groups from Russian‑occupied territories moving toward Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Sumy and Odesa regions. Russian forces have seized Novovasylivka in Kharkiv and threaten Iskander‑M missile launches from Voronezh. Sweden pledges 16 Gripen fighters for delivery in 2027, while Ukraine's defense budget receives a record boost.

points: The Verkhovna Rada approved a record defense budget increase of 1.56 trillion hryvnias, allocating funds for personnel pay, weapons procurement and equipment modernization, financed through EU external assistance (Source: @UkraineNow/81510).
Ukraine plans to receive 16 JAS 39 C/D Gripen fighters from Sweden, with deliveries expected at the start of 2027, enhancing air defence and overall aerial capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81512).
Ukrainian authorities denied reports that the U.S. embassy would leave Ukraine amid Russian threats of intensified strikes on Kyiv (Source: @UkraineNow/81507).
Ukrainian Foreign Ministry head Andrey Sibiga emphasized Ukraine and Europe hold "trump cards" for peace negotiations, indicating diplomatic leverage (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30498).
The Rada voted on a bill to ratify a 90 billion‑euro EU loan agreement, including tax measures to support financing (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30493).
Russian forces reported control over Novovasylivka in Kharkiv oblast, expanding their foothold in the region (Source: @tass_agency/377951).
A threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Voronezh oblast was highlighted, indicating potential long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67252).
Russian officials warned that a strike on Kyiv, previously mentioned by Shoigu, could be carried out at any moment (Source: @tass_agency/377977).
Russia announced plans to limit fruit and vegetable imports from Armenia starting 30 May, reflecting broader economic measures (Source: @tass_agency/377951).
Russian media reported the development of a new set of reagents for detecting avian influenza A(H5N8) within the country (Source: @tass_agency/377991).
UAVs from the Black Sea are heading toward southern Odesa direction, indicating pressure on the eastern coastal front (Source: @kpszsu/63140).
Groups of UAVs from Kursk region (Russia) are directed toward Sumy oblast, suggesting cross‑border aerial incursions (Source: @kpszsu/63132).
UAV activity reported from the north of Kharkiv oblast heading toward Chuhuiv, a strategic town on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/63140).
New UAV groups observed from the north of Chernihiv oblast moving toward Kyiv region, potentially targeting the capital's outskirts (Source: @kpszsu/63140).
UAVs from the north of Kharkiv oblast are reported heading toward the city of Kharkiv itself (Source: @eRadarrua/94063).
UAVs from the north of Chernihiv oblast are heading toward Mena and Makoshyne, indicating activity near the Belarus border (Source: @kpszsu/63169).
UAVs from the north of Poltava oblast are moving toward Dykanka, showing northern operational reach (Source: @kpszsu/63104).
UAVs from the north of Kharkiv oblast are reported heading toward Chuhuiv, a key northern target (Source: @kpszsu/63104).
A group of UAVs from Bryansk region (Russia) is directed northward into Chernihiv oblast (Source: @kpszsu/63124).
UAVs from the north of Chernihiv oblast are also reported heading toward Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/63140).
UAV groups from Nikopol are operating in the south and west, indicating heightened activity in the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/63128).
UAVs from the south of Odesa oblast are heading toward Izmail, showing pressure on the Danube corridor (Source: @kpszsu/63140).
UAVs from the south of Sumy oblast are heading toward Lebedyn and Lipova Valley, suggesting southern thrusts in the east (Source: @kpszsu/63136).
UAVs from the Black Sea are moving toward the southern coast and Odesa direction (Source: @kpszsu/63140).
UAVs from the south of Dnipropetrovsk region are heading toward Kryvyi Rih and Chaplyne, indicating southern operational focus (Source: @kpszsu/63132).
Sweden will supply Ukraine with 16 Gripen fighters, enhancing air superiority and defence capabilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81512).
Russian threats of an Iskander‑M missile launch from Voronezh underscore the risk of long‑range aerial attacks (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67252).
UAV activity is widespread across multiple fronts, with groups from Russian regions targeting Ukrainian towns, reflecting intensified drone warfare (Source: @kpszsu/63140).
A MiG‑31K equipped with an X‑47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile was reported to have taken off, prompting a nationwide air‑raid alert (Source: @eRadarrua/94070).
UAVs over the Black Sea were reported targeting oil tanker operations, indicating a maritime air threat component (Source: @UkraineNow/81518).

key_developments: UAV groups from Russian‑occupied territories are operating across northern, eastern and southern Ukrainian oblasts, targeting towns such as Mena, Chuhuiv, Lebedyn and Izmail (Source: @kpszsu/63140).
Russian forces have seized control of Novovasylivka in Kharkiv region, expanding their ground presence (Source: @tass_agency/377951).
A threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Voronezh oblast was announced, indicating potential long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67252).
Sweden committed to delivering 16 Gripen fighters to Ukraine, with deliveries slated for early 2027, bolstering Ukrainian air power (Source: @UkraineNow/81512).
Ukraine's defense budget was increased by 1.56 trillion hryvnias, funded through EU assistance, to support personnel, weapons procurement and equipment upgrades (Source: @UkraineNow/81510).
UAVs from the Black Sea are heading toward the southern coast and Odesa direction, suggesting maritime‑linked aerial operations (Source: @kpszsu/63140).
Ukrainian authorities denied reports of a U.S. embassy withdrawal amid Russian threats of intensified Kyiv strikes (Source: @UkraineNow/81507).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian UAV incursions from multiple directions increase pressure on Ukrainian command and control, especially in border oblasts. The reported Iskander‑M missile threat from Voronezh adds a strategic long‑range danger to Ukrainian air defences. Control of Novovasylivka gives Russia a foothold for further eastern operations, while the MiG‑31K alert underscores heightened aerial threat levels.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is bolstering its defence budget and seeking advanced air platforms, exemplified by the Gripen deal with Sweden, to counter the expanding UAV and missile threats. International partners, notably the EU, are providing financial support to sustain these efforts. Russian statements about possible Kyiv strikes aim to exert psychological pressure but have not yet translated into confirmed large‑scale attacks.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continuous monitoring of UAV flight paths from Russian and occupied territories is essential for early warning and interception. The Iskander‑M threat requires heightened missile defence readiness, particularly in central and western Ukraine. Coordination with NATO allies on air‑defence asset deployment will be critical as the aerial threat environment intensifies.
